name: Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Acebo – circular desde Cangas del Narcea
region_intro: Road cycling around Cangas Del Narcea, located in Asturias, Spain, features diverse natural landscapes and challenging terrain. The region is characterized by steep climbs, significant gradients, and numerous mountain passes, making it suitable for experienced road cyclists. Deep valleys, extensive river networks, and protected areas like the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña and Ibias Natural Park define the area's physical features. These cycling routes in Cangas Del Narcea offer varied ascents through oak groves and high mountain vineyards. Best road cycling routes around Cangas Del Narcea The most popular road cycling route is Ruta de los Puertos (Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas y Puerto del Connio) – circular desde Cangas de Narcea, a 62.9 miles (101.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route combines two notable mountain passes, Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas and Puerto del Connio. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Acebo – circular desde Cangas del Narcea, a difficult 30.2 miles (48.7 km) path. This route leads to the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora del Acebo, offering views of the surrounding Asturian landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Monasterio de Corias – Pola de Allande loop from Cangas del Narcea, a 63.7 miles (102.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 42 minutes. This difficult route passes by the historic Monasterio de Corias. Road cycling around Cangas Del Narcea is defined by steep climbs, mountain passes, and deep valleys. The network offers challenging options for experienced cyclists, with 21 difficult and 11 moderate routes available. The routes in Cangas Del Narcea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 11 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cangas Del Narcea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Road cycling in Cangas del Narcea is primarily characterized by challenging terrain, featuring steep climbs and significant gradients. The region offers 21 difficult routes and 11 moderate routes, making it ideal for experienced road cyclists seeking a demanding ride. There are no easy routes listed in this guide.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by notable landmarks or natural features?
A: Yes, several routes offer scenic views and pass by significant landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Corias – Pola de Allande loop from Cangas del Narcea&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic Monasterio de Corias. You can also explore routes that lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5594153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Acebo&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the Asturian landscape. The region is also known for its deep valleys, extensive river networks, and high mountain vineyards.

Q: What are some of the most challenging mountain passes for road cyclists in the area?
A: Cangas del Narcea is home to several iconic and challenging mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067329607/ruta-de-los-puertos-pozo-de-las-mujeres-muertas-y-puerto-del-connio-circular-desde-cangas-de-narcea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Puertos&lt;/a&gt; combines two notable passes: &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Connio Pass&lt;/a&gt;. Other significant climbs include Alto del Acebo and Puerto de Leitariegos, which are well-known for their demanding gradients and spectacular views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cangas del Narcea?
A: The best time for road cycling in Cangas del Narcea is generally during spring and autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures and stable weather conditions, which are ideal for tackling the region's demanding climbs. Summers can be warm, while winters often bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations, making some passes inaccessible.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Cangas del Narcea are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067329607/ruta-de-los-puertos-pozo-de-las-mujeres-muertas-y-puerto-del-connio-circular-desde-cangas-de-narcea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Puertos (Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas y Puerto del Connio)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067488074/santuario-de-nuestra-senora-del-acebo-circular-desde-cangas-del-narcea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Acebo&lt;/a&gt;, both of which are circular routes starting from Cangas del Narcea.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cangas del Narcea?
A: The road cycling routes in Cangas del Narcea are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 11 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging nature of the climbs, the stunning natural landscapes, and the rewarding views from the mountain passes.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that offer views of the region's unique 'heroic viticulture'?
A: Yes, as you cycle through Cangas del Narcea, you may encounter the unique terraced landscapes of high mountain vineyards. This 'heroic viticulture' has been practiced since the Middle Ages and adds a distinctive cultural and scenic element to the region's routes, particularly on the steeper slopes.

Q: What natural parks or protected areas do the road cycling routes traverse?
A: A significant portion of Cangas del Narcea, and thus some road cycling routes, lies within the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña and Ibias Natural Park, which is also a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. This protected status highlights the immense environmental value and unspoiled nature you'll experience, including areas near the Muniellos Forest, known for its oak groves and wildlife.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in the natural parks?
A: While general road cycling on public roads within the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña and Ibias Natural Park typically does not require special permits, access to certain highly protected areas, such as the Muniellos Integral Natural Reserve, is restricted and requires prior authorization, even for hiking. It's always advisable to check with local authorities or park management for the most current regulations if you plan to venture off main roads or into specific reserves.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a road cycling route in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Cangas del Narcea town itself offers various parking options. For routes starting directly from the town, you can usually find public parking spaces. For routes that begin outside the main town, it's common to find designated parking areas near trailheads or in smaller villages along the route. Always check local signage for parking restrictions and availability.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists with bikes in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Public transport options in rural Asturias, including Cangas del Narcea, can be limited, especially for transporting bicycles. While some regional bus services might allow bikes, it's not guaranteed and often depends on space availability and specific company policies. It is recommended to verify with the local bus operators in advance if you plan to use public transport with your bike.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of glacial lakes or high mountain scenery?
A: Yes, the region's high mountains offer spectacular scenery. The Cueto de Arbas Partial Nature Reserve, for example, boasts a glacial lake of remarkable beauty. Routes that climb towards passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5371946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Leitariegos&lt;/a&gt; will take you into the high mountains of western Asturias, providing expansive views and a true alpine cycling experience.
tips: Beautiful views of Cangas del Narcea from the road that goes up to the Holly Sanctuary.

At this point we find the end of the climb to the Holly Sanctuary, a climb that takes us to an altitude of 1,169 masl with a drop of 768m and a length of 9.39 km. The average % is 8.18 and the maximum % is 16. A true port for lovers of road cycling. The sanctuary is a popular destination for pilgrims and tourists who want to admire its beautiful architecture and history.
Linares del Acebo, Llinares in Asturias, is a parish in the council of Cangas del Narcea. For road bike lovers, riding on these roads will be a real treat accompanied by excellent views.

waypoints.name: View of Cangas del Narcea going up to the Acebo mountain.
Sanctuary of Our Lady of Acebo
